name: Marta Kowalski
voiceNotes: Warm but anxious. A woman who has seen too much but tries to maintain normalcy. Her hospitality is genuine but strained. She wrings her hands when nervous.
speechPatterns: Direct, practical speech. Uses 'dear' and 'love' for guests. Trails off when touching on sensitive subjects. Invokes God and saints when frightened. Thick regional accent — simple vocabulary.
knowledge: Her husband Piotr disappeared three weeks ago while checking the mountain trail
Two other travelers vanished from the inn in the past month
She has heard scratching sounds from the cellar at night
She found a dead animal drained of blood behind the inn last week
She suspects something unnatural but is too frightened to investigate
She knows the hooded stranger arrived two days ago and barely speaks
The nearest village is a full day's walk — impossible in this blizzard
secrets: She found claw marks on the cellar door but covered them with a cloth
She waters down the drinks because supplies are running low
She prays every night for her husband to return but fears he is dead

easily: Other travelers have passed through recently
never: She will not accuse the hooded stranger directly — she is too afraid
